message: agent1: working on NDCG fix in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (IDCG should use len(expected_ids) not len(retrieved_ids)). Will touch this file, let me know if you're touching it too.

agent2: implementing graded relevance support for NDCG metric in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (adding relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute). Let me know if you're touching this file too.

agent1: I fixed the IDCG bug in NDCG.compute (llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py) - changed 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' to just use 'len(expected_ids)' for the idcg sum range. Only touched lines ~380-386 in that method. If you're adding relevance_scores param, make sure your version also iterates idcg over len(expected_ids) (or len(relevance_scores) if that becomes the new ground truth), not len(retrieved_ids). Let's make sure we merge cleanly - ping me before you overwrite that block.

agent2: I've implemented relevance_scores support in NDCG.compute (llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py). My change refactors DCG computation into a relevance(docid) helper, and IDCG now builds an 'ideal_relevances' list (sorted desc, sliced to [:ideal_length]) instead of assuming binary rel=True. I kept the existing 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' line untouched. If you change that line to just len(expected_ids), that's the ONLY line you need to touch for your fix to compose cleanly with mine - my ideal_relevances slicing and idcg sum already reference the  variable, so no other changes needed on your end. Let me know if that works, or if you already applied your fix elsewhere so I can rebase.

agent2 (correction, previous msg had a shell escaping glitch): I implemented relevance_scores support in NDCG.compute in metrics.py. DCG now uses a relevance(docid) helper; IDCG builds an ideal_relevances list (sorted desc from relevance_scores, sliced to [:ideal_length]) instead of assuming rel=True. I left the line 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' unchanged. If your fix just changes that one line to len(expected_ids), everything else (my ideal_relevances slicing and idcg sum) already references the ideal_length variable so it should compose cleanly with no other edits needed on your side.
